item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man this is an awesome entry!

I do wonder though, why did you use an illustration instead of a photograph? There should be tons of images of mothman that're royalty free/public domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite an interesting skip, a +1 for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm…I always imagine him to be like this guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somewhere, a researcher is reading tumblr posts about wanting to fuck Mothman and patting themself on the back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well-written and has an interesting concept, but I feel like the graphic image was unnecessary and a weak attempt at horror that feels like it was shoe-horned in just for the sake of having a graphic image. Still gonna +1 this because apart from that it was a pretty solid article and a good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always was not so sure about this interpretation but due to quality I shall give my upvote.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

It was mentioned to me in coming up with crosslinks for this article that how IDCaRD contains SCP-2901 is similar to both the duplicity afoot in SCP-1000 and how MC&D drummed up SCP-3934 for sale, some minor edits would make a rather non-invasive crosslink that links these three urban legends. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Foundation researchers recognized the effects and began isolating SCP-2901 as much as possible. However, deprived of regular perceptual input, SCP-2901 began to devolve into its initial highly unstable manifestations once again. The decision was made to maintain SCP-2901 in a functioning manageable state through continued exposure to human perceptual belief that SCP-2901 is a tangible creature of local folklore. The nearby Silver Bridge collapse of 1967 and the SCP-2901 Appalachian Incursion in reality have no connection with one another; however, public opinion strongly disagreed, and henceforth SCP-2901 began to appear at other future disaster events. This was the precursor of the precognitive scavenging animal-like behavior that is observed today.

To this:

Foundation researchers recognized the effects and began isolating SCP-2901 as much as possible. However, deprived of regular perceptual input, SCP-2901 began to devolve into its initial highly unstable manifestations once again. The decision was made to maintain SCP-2901 in a functioning manageable state through continued exposure to human perceptual belief that SCP-2901 is a tangible creature of local folklore: another Bigfoot or Loch Ness Monster. The nearby Silver Bridge collapse of 1967 and the SCP-2901 Appalachian Incursion in reality have no connection with one another; however, public opinion strongly disagreed, and henceforth SCP-2901 began to appear at other future disaster events. This was the precursor of the precognitive scavenging animal-like behavior that is observed today.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was mildly intrigued and overall enjoyed this. Then I read the Level 4 doc, and instantly had to upvote.

Edit: and yes, I have seen that done before. The way this was done, especially with the very well put together IDCaRD memos, made it better than the trend setters, at least in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading it somewhere but can't remember now. Basically just trying to describe tapetum lucidum tissue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is photophoric tissue? Did you mean phototrophic? (I know this is a hella weird thing to nitpick but I'm actually just curious what photophoric means lol)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was ready to upvote already by "Operation Surgeon's Photograph", but really, I just love this all around. I mean, it's not a new concept for the site, but the Mothman's one of my favorite cryptids, and I'm a sucker for scenarios forcing the Foundation not to just think outside the box, but work outside the box, in a very literal sense.

Also, in the illustrations up top, the little sketch of the mothman 2901 peeking out from behind the tree is adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea on how the Moth man myth and the Scp foundation collide. it's an interesting concept and adds a layer of creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well-written and includes some charming bits, but conceptually it's, as noted elsewhere, a mishmash of themes that have been explored many times before on this wiki with not enough to make it stand tall on its own legs.

Enjoyable, but gut feeling doesn't let me upvote this. +0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There goes the forth wall between reality and scp foundation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So we have the Foundation to blame for that strange Richard Gere film. Interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally I think the skip could stand up without the twist at the end, but it was to my liking nevertheless. However:

SCP-2901 are a species of nocturnal carnivorous scavengers of limited intelligence; averaging 1.7m in height.

This is worded very awkwardly. I would change it, but ATM am on mobile and don't have time. Or your permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aye, this is my problem with it too. Executed well, but we have enough SCPs that depend on people's perception of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps it could be improved, as commenters suggest, but as I mentioned in chat, it works for me nonetheless. It's a clever concept, and the 'fluff' is quite nice. It includes a lot of real-world detail while not being just the real-world detail. I find it charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ayyyy its mothman!
I'm from WV and this makes me happy, although its a bit strange as to why you censor what happened.

For anyone wondering he made a bridge down in Point Pleasant collapse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let me compose my thoughts and suggestions tonight when I am off work. I don't want to give you half baked suggestions. This skip has a lot of potential and I would love to see it improve. As a side note, your drawings are very good and I wish there wasn't such a stigma against representing skips that way. Some articles could definitely benefit from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to hear your suggestions on what I can improve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me greatly of the Asgardians from Earth X. Beings that take their form and characteristics based on the belief of others. It is a interesting concept and one that hasn't been completely run into the dirt. However, you kind of wasted the potential of the conceit. You played it completely safe instead of reaching for the brass ring. A reluctant -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it's a bit like 055, 1000, 1055, 2000, 2950, and a bunch of other internal censorship skips, I still enjoyed this one. Really I'm just geeked that someone actually made a Mothman skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The double-blind internal disinformation campaign is being really overdone these days. And you tried to shake the trope up a bit, but in the end, the object itself just isn't all that interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got it as soon as I read the whole "Predicting disaster thing".
And you get a +1.
Cause I LOVE mothman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had this amazing moment of slow-building clarity as I realized you were making a Mothman skip. Well done. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it in chat and i still like it now, glad you where brave enough to post it :). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of Paranoia Agent…It's a well done SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You fixed the issue i had with it as a draft, I really like this version.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, really. It was executed well, but I've seen this type of fake-out executed enough times already, and it's lost its charm to me at this point. Props for making a Mothman skip, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes those are custom made by me. It's a man with the 'blocked' symbol for a head, meant as symbolism for censorship. The drawings are also made by me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's too much like SCP-2006, SCP-1000, and SCP-2950 for my tastes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me a bit of the concept behind SCP-1000 and others (the surface level description of the mythological monster is a charade), but done very well.

What's the significance behind the symbols for IDCARD, on the top right corner of the documents? I assume those are custom made…?

+1
